title: Premium
text: A premium is the amount you pay every month for your health insurance plan, whether or not you use medical services.

title: Deductible
text: A deductible is the amount you pay for covered health care services before your insurance plan starts to pay. For example, with a 2500 dollar deductible, you pay the first 2500 dollars of covered services yourself.

title: Out-of-pocket maximum
text: The out-of-pocket maximum is the most you have to pay for covered services in a plan year. After you reach this amount, the insurance plan pays 100 percent of covered benefits.

title: Metal level
text: Metal levels such as Bronze, Silver, Gold, and Platinum describe how costs are shared between you and the insurance plan. Bronze usually has lower premiums and higher costs when you get care. Gold usually has higher premiums and lower costs when you get care.

title: Choosing a plan
text: A low-premium plan may be cheaper each month but can have a higher deductible. A higher-premium plan may be better for someone who expects frequent medical care.
